name: Margarita Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Mix cookie crumbs and butter in a medium bowl until blended. Press the mixture onto the bottom and 1-inch up the sides of a 9-inch diameter springform pan with 2-3/4 inch high sides. Refrigerate while preparing the filling.
3. Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese in a large bowl until fluffy. Add 1 cup sour cream, 1 cup sugar, Grand Marnier, tequila, lime juice, and lime peel; beat until well blended.
4. Add eggs one at a time, beating just until blended after each addition.
5. Pour the filling into the crust. Bake until the center is softly set, about 50 minutes. Maintain oven temperature.
6. Mix remaining 1 cup sour cream, 1/4 cup sugar, and 1 tablespoon lime juice in a small bowl. Pour over cheesecake. Using a spatula, smooth the top. Bake for 5 minutes longer.
7. Transfer pan to a rack and cool completely. Refrigerate until well chilled, at least 4 hours or overnight.
8. Run a knife around the pan sides to loosen the cake. Remove the pan sides. Garnish the cake with lime slices.
Yield: 10 to 12 servings
